---
title: "5.1.1 The differences between the internet and the World Wide Web (WWW)"
subtitle: "The internet and the World Wide Web · P180-P181"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        .reveal .figure-note { font-size: 0.82em; line-height: 1.28; }
        .reveal .figure-placeholder { border: 1px dashed #8aa0bf; border-radius: 8px; background: #f4f8ff; color: #40526b; padding: 1.1em 1.3em; margin: 0.8em 0; font-size: 0.9em; text-align: center; }
        .reveal .clean-table table { font-size: 0.72em; line-height: 1.1; }
        .reveal .clean-table th, .reveal .clean-table td { padding: 0.24em 0.34em; vertical-align: top; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 5 Directory / 返回 Unit 5 目录](../../index.html)

## Internet

### INTERconnected NETwork

The word <span class="term">internet</span> (互联网) comes from <span class="mark">INTERconnected NETwork</span>, since it is basically a worldwide collection of interconnected networks.

The internet is actually a <span class="mark">concept rather than something tangible</span>. It relies on a physical infrastructure that allows networks and individual devices to connect to other networks and devices.

## World Wide Web

### A part of the internet

In contrast, the <span class="term">World Wide Web (WWW)</span> (万维网) is only a part of the internet that users can access using <span class="term">web browser</span> software.

The World Wide Web consists of a massive collection of web pages, and is based on the <span class="term">hypertext transfer protocol</span>.

## Core Difference

### Accessing information using the internet

The <span class="term">World Wide Web</span> is a way of accessing information using the <span class="term">internet</span>.

Therefore, the internet and the World Wide Web are actually quite different.

## Table 5.1

### Internet

::: {.clean-table}
| Internet |
|---|
| users can send and receive emails |
| allows online chatting via text, audio and video |
| makes use of transmission protocols (TCP) and internet protocols (IP) |
| it is a worldwide collection of interconnected networks and devices |
:::

## Table 5.1

### World Wide Web 1/2

::: {.clean-table}
| World Wide Web (WWW) |
|---|
| it is a collection of multimedia web pages and other information on websites |
| http(s) protocols are written using hypertext mark-up language (HTML) |
| uniform resource locators (URLs) are used to specify the location of web pages |
:::

## Table 5.1

### World Wide Web 2/2

::: {.clean-table}
| World Wide Web (WWW) |
|---|
| web resources are accessed by web browsers |
| uses the internet to access information from web servers |
:::

## Classroom Check

### Internet vs WWW

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- the internet is a <span class="mark">worldwide collection of interconnected networks and devices</span>
- the internet relies on physical infrastructure but is a concept rather than one tangible object
- the World Wide Web is <span class="mark">only a part of the internet</span>
- the WWW consists of web pages and uses web browsers
- the WWW uses http(s), HTML and URLs to access information from web servers
:::

## Bridge

### Next: URLs

The next deck explains how <span class="term">uniform resource locators</span> specify the location of web pages.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 5 Directory / 返回 Unit 5 目录](../../index.html)
